description.p: Before the Severing, the Vaelorin were skybound urban elves who dwelled within the great central kingdom at the height of its arcane-technological brilliance. While other peoples filled the streets and towers, the Vaelorin claimed the rooftops.

They designed and maintained elevated gardens, vertical orchards, wind-harvesting terraces, and living skybridges woven with vine and spellcraft. They moved fluidly across the city's upper levels, treating rooftops and suspended walkways as others treated forest canopies. To them, architecture was terrain.

Their magic focused on cultivated growth—controlled ecosystems sustained through careful arcane and botanical mastery. They were planners, terrace farmers, skyline pathfinders, and quiet guardians of breathable spaces.

Though fully integrated into urban society, they favored open air and elevation over enclosed chambers. Even before the Severing, they were defined by movement. In crowded streets, they seemed distant. Above it all, they were exactly where they belonged.

When the Weaver severed divine connection and destabilized arcane systems, urban infrastructure faltered. Irrigation enchantments failed. Skybridges collapsed. Vertical gardens withered without the magic that sustained them.
The Vaelorin adapted.

Survivors scattered beyond the ruined kingdom, carrying knowledge of cultivation, navigation, and environmental harmony. In the centuries since, they have become wilderness pathfinders, migratory orchard-keepers, monster hunters, survivalists, animal tamers, and horizon-watchers.

Modern Vaelorin distrust confinement and fragile systems. They favor open skies, plains, highlands, coasts, and forest edges—places where visibility and mobility ensure survival. Their settlements are often temporary or seasonal, leaving minimal scars upon the land.

Their philosophy centers on resilience through motion. They plant knowing they may not remain. They travel knowing the path matters more than the destination. They do not linger long enough for the world to depend on them. And they are careful never to depend on it in return.
Vaelorin are lean and long-limbed, built for endurance and balance rather than ornament. Their movements are deliberate and economical, conserving energy even in stillness.

Their ears are noticeably longer than those of most elven lineages, tapering into pronounced, blade-like points that sweep subtly backward. Among the Vaelorin, ear length is often regarded as a mark of lineage and perceptiveness.

Most striking are their eyes. Nearly all Vaelorin possess the gaze of a predator—vertical slit pupils set within irises that often carry a faint inner luminosity. Eye colors commonly include deep emerald, amber, gold, storm-gray, or muted violet. In rare cases, individuals display unusual patterns such as fractured color bands, radial striations, or dual-toned gradients.
To meet the eyes of a Vaelorin is to feel measured. They do not look at the world casually. They assess it.
Your Vaelorin character has the following racial traits.

Ability Score Increase. Increase one ability score by 2 and a different ability score by 1, or increase three different ability scores by 1.
Age. Vaelorin mature at a rate similar to humans, reaching physical adulthood in their early twenties. However, they are not considered full members of their people until completing a formative period of travel and self-sufficiency, typically between the ages of 40 and 50. Vaelorin commonly live between 400 and 500 years, though few in the modern age reach the heights of longevity recorded before the Weaver's Touch.
Size. Vaelorin have the slender build typical of elves. Your size is Medium.
Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.
Darkvision. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness.
Keen Senses. You have proficiency in the Perception skill.
Fey Ancestry. You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.
Trance. You meditate deeply for 4 hours instead of sleeping, gaining the same benefit as 8 hours of rest, remaining semiconscious.
Elf Weapon Training. You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.
Cultivator's Cantrip. You know one cantrip of your choice from the druid spell list. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.
Extra Language. You can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choice.
Wanderer. You have excellent memory for maps and geography, recalling terrain and settlements. You can also find food and fresh water for yourself and up to five others each day, provided resources are available.
Predator's Eye. Your eyes are trained to catch the smallest details—once to navigate impossible paths, now to survive a fractured world. As a bonus action, you focus your senses on a creature you can see within 60 feet. You instantly assess its condition and defenses. You learn whether the creature is above or below half its maximum hit points, as well as its Armor Class. You then gain advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness.

sheet.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

description.p: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

sheet.description: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

description.p: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

sheet.description: Advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

description.p: You meditate deeply for 4 hours instead of sleeping, gaining the same benefit as 8 hours of rest, remaining semiconscious.

sheet.description: You meditate for 4 hours instead of sleeping, gaining the same benefit as 8 hours of rest.

description.p: You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

sheet.description: You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

description.p: You know one cantrip of your choice from the druid spell list. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

sheet.description: You know one cantrip of your choice from the druid spell list. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

description.p: You can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choice.

sheet.description: You can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choice.

description.p: You have excellent memory for maps and geography, recalling terrain and settlements. You can also find food and fresh water for yourself and up to five others each day, provided resources are available.

sheet.description: Excellent memory for maps and geography. Can find food and fresh water for yourself and up to five others each day, provided resources are available.

description.p: Your eyes are trained to catch the smallest details—once to navigate impossible paths, now to survive a fractured world.

As a bonus action, you focus your senses on a creature you can see within 60 feet. You instantly assess its condition and defenses. You learn whether the creature is above or below half its maximum hit points, as well as its Armor Class. You then gain advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

sheet.description: Bonus action: learn a creature's HP threshold and AC within 60 feet, then gain advantage on your next attack roll against it. 1/long rest.
